---
title: ""
title-block: false
page-layout: full
---

::::: home-layout
::: home-left
       
<img src="images/myphoto.jpg" alt="Md Nazmul Islam" class="profile-photo">

<h2>Md Nazmul Islam</h2>
<p>MS Student in Medicine</p>

<div class="profile-links">
  <p><i class="fa-solid fa-location-dot"></i> Bangladesh</p>
  <p><a href="https://bau.edu.bd/" target="_blank"><i class="fa-solid fa-building-columns"></i> Bangladesh Agricultural University</a></p>
  <p><a href="mailto:nazmul.1901007@bau.edu.bd"><i class="fa-solid fa-envelope"></i> Email</a></p>
  <p><a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/md-nazmul-islam-edu/" target="_blank"><i class="fa-brands fa-linkedin"></i> LinkedIn</a></p>
  <p><a href="https://github.com/nazmulislammd" target="_blank"><i class="fa-brands fa-github"></i> GitHub</a></p>
  <p><a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=znn6vX8AAAAJ" target="_blank"><i class="fa-solid fa-graduation-cap"></i> Google Scholar</a></p>
  <p><a href="https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Md-Nazmul-Islam-26" target="_blank"><i class="fa-solid fa-flask"></i> ResearchGate</a></p>
  <p><a href="https://orcid.org/0009-0002-1752-9210" target="_blank"><i class="fa-brands fa-orcid"></i> ORCID</a></p>
</div>

:::

::: home-right
<p class="hero-title">Veterinary epidemiologist building Bayesian models for zoonotic disease surveillance and control in dairy systems.</p>

<p>I am a Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) graduate and an MS candidate in Medicine at the Department of Medicine, Bangladesh Agricultural University.</p>

<p>My research focuses on veterinary epidemiology, zoonotic infectious diseases in livestock, and One Health approaches, integrating Bayesian statistical modeling, field diagnostics, and epidemiological risk analysis to support improved disease surveillance and control in dairy production systems.</p>

<p>Working under the supervision of <a href="https://vm.bau.edu.bd/teachers/VM1004" target="_blank"><strong>A.K.M. Anisur Rahman</strong></a>, I combine practical field and laboratory experience with advanced analytical tools such as R for Bayesian analysis, ELISA/qPCR diagnostics, and QGIS-based spatial analysis to produce robust, reproducible epidemiological evidence.</p>

<div class="thesis-card">
  <span class="thesis-eyebrow">Current Research · MS Thesis (In Progress)</span>
  <p class="thesis-title">Transmission Dynamics, Intervention Simulation, and Economic Burden of Q Fever and Neosporosis in Large-Scale Commercial Dairy Herds of Bangladesh: A Hierarchical Bayesian Approach</p>
  <p class="thesis-meta">Master of Science in Medicine · April 2025 – Present · Department of Medicine, Bangladesh Agricultural University<br>
  Supervisor: <a href="https://vm.bau.edu.bd/teachers/VM1004" target="_blank">Prof. Dr. A.K.M. Anisur Rahman</a> · CGPA 3.80 / 4.00 (first semester)</p>
</div>

<p>You can explore my publications, working papers, research projects, public engagement activities, and teaching contributions by navigating the sections of this website.</p>

<h2>Key Highlights</h2>
<ul>
  <li><strong>Seven peer-reviewed journal articles</strong> published, including three as first or equally contributing first author; <strong>one additional article accepted and in press</strong> in <em>PLOS ONE</em>; and <strong>seven manuscripts under review</strong> in journals including <em>Preventive Veterinary Medicine</em>, <em>Veterinary Medicine and Science</em>, and <em>Epidemics</em>.</li>
  <li>Applied <strong>Bayesian prevalence estimation, catalytic transmission modelling, and risk-factor analysis</strong> to brucellosis, paratuberculosis, Q fever, neosporosis, bovine viral diarrhoea, and subclinical mastitis in large-scale Bangladeshi dairy herds.</li>
  <li><strong>Leading an independent global epidemiology programme on human brucellosis</strong>, applying Bayesian meta-synthesis, spatiotemporal mapping, and One Health frameworks with a multidisciplinary team.</li>
  <li>Recipient of the <strong>National Science and Technology (NST) Fellowship 2026</strong>.</li>
</ul>

<h2>Research Interests</h2>
<ul>
  <li>Veterinary epidemiology and zoonotic/infectious disease dynamics</li>
  <li>Bayesian and quantitative epidemiological modelling</li>
  <li>Diagnostic-test evaluation and disease surveillance</li>
  <li>Dairy herd health and production-impact assessment</li>
  <li>One Health approaches in South Asian and LMIC settings</li>
</ul>
:::
:::::
